description_long: This is a commercial passenger airplane with a streamlined fuselage painted primarily in white and blue, featuring the livery 'SIMPLE AIRWAYS' along the side. The aircraft has two large turbofan engines mounted under each wing, which are swept back and equipped with small winglets at the tips. The tail section is a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The landing gear is visible and consists of a tricycle configuration with two main gear units and a nose gear. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the flight crew. The overall design suggests a modern, mid-sized jet airliner suitable for urban airports.
description: A modern commercial jet airliner with blue and white livery, swept-back wings with winglets, twin underwing turbofan engines, and a conventional tail. The airplane features a forward cockpit and tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, winglets, and the blue tail fin, with both engines and landing gear visible.
description_view_1: Left side profile highlighting the 'SIMPLE AIRWAYS' livery, engine placement, and the vertical stabilizer with red markings.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the nose, cockpit windows, both engines, and the symmetry of the wings and landing gear.
description_view_3: Right side profile mirroring the left, showing the engine, landing gear, and tail design, with clear view of the blue and red tail fin.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back wings with small upward winglets at the tips and slight dihedral angle.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
